semanticCore: Sea, targeting, dust, intending, direction, or mother
quranicRelevance: Deep sea/water body (Yamm - baby Moses or Pharaoh's army, 'fal-taltamisu fee al-yammi'), and intending clean soil for ablution (Tayammum - 'fatayammamoo sa'eedan tayyiban').

keyDerivations.meaning: Sea, open water, river (e.g. Nile), or deep water
To intend, aim at, target, or perform tayammum (aim for soil)

figurativeOrRareUsages: أَنِ اقْذِفِيهِ فِي التَّابُوتِ فَاقْذِفِيهِ فِي الْيَمِّ (20:39): Throw him into the chest, then throw him into the river/sea (Yamm representing the Nile).
discardedNoise: The right direction (Tayamun) - belonging to y-m-n.
summaryEn: The root covers water and intention, representing the Nile water (Yamm) of Moses' rescue, Pharaoh's drowning, and the dry ablution intention (Tayammum).
summaryAr: يغطي هذا الجذر اليم (البحر) والقصد، ممثلاً مياه النيل المنقذة لرضيع موسى (اليم)، وغرق فرعون، والتيمم الترابي القاصد للطهور.
rawText: الفَرِيضَةُ اليَمِّيَّةُ : see المُشَرَّكَةُ. تَيَمَّمَ 5 : see صَعِيدٌ and art. ام. === تَيَمَّمَ يَمَامَةً i. q. قَصَدَ قَصْدًا. (TA in art. ام.) يَمَامٌ : see حَمَامٌ. يَمَّمَ 2 : see أَمَّمَ. And يَمَّمَ المَرِيضَ and المَيِّتَ: see Mgh in art. أَمَّ.
